title: MG-132 (Invitrogen, #tlrl-mg132-2) and hydroxychloroquine (Sigma, #H0915-5MG) assay
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7bbmk","text":"Protocol for evaluating the role for various protein degradation pathways on the half-life of TBK1 N455S","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"5ptgo","text":"COS-1 cells","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ed1e1","text":"Cycloheximide (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Catalog #AC357420010)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7nhbg","text":"Dimethyl Sulfoxide (DMSO), filtered ()","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f3p5k","text":"RNAse free H2O, filter (Quality biological, #351-029-721)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":12,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"591sj","text":"M-PER Mammalian Protein Extraction Reagent (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Catalog #78505)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9vul1","text":"Halt Protease Inhibitor Cocktail, EDTA-free (100X) (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Catalog #78425)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7k3hj","text":"MG-132 (Invitrogen, #tlrl-mg132-2)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6srj5","text":"Hydroxychloroquine (Sigma, #H0915-5MG)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Follow the protocol for the cycloheximide assay, treating with CHX + MG-132 OR HCQ at time points (READ PROTOCOL-SPECIFIC PARAMETERS BELOW BEFORE BEGINNING). Cycloheximide assay (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Catalog #AC357420010) by Christopher Bartley Protocol-specific parameters: There will be 3 conditions: (1) CHX alone, (2) CHX + MG132, and (3) CHX + HCQ). Therefore, For Step 4 you will, (1) Add 20 µL of 10 mg/mL CHX (for a final concentration in the wells of 100 ug/ml CHX) + 20 µL H 2 O (filtered) to the 24-hour time point, CHX alone wells only. (2) add 20 µL of 10 mg/mL CHX + 20 µL of 2 millimolar (mM) MG-132 to the 24-hour time point, CHX + MG-132 wells only. (3) add 20 µL of 10 mg/mL CHX + 20 µL of 5 millimolar (mM) HCQ to the 24-hour time point, CHX + HCQ wells only. For Step 5 you will, (1) Add 20 µL of 10 mg/mL CHX (for a final concentration of 10 ug/ml CHX) + 20 µL, H2O to the 16-hour time point, CHX alone wells only. (2) add 20 µL of 10 mg/mL CHX + 20 µL of 2 millimolar (mM) MG-132 to the 16-hour time point, CHX + MG-132 wells only. (3) add 20 µL of 10 mg/mL CHX + 20 µL of 5 millimolar (mM) HCQ to the 16-hour time point, CHX + HCQ wells only.
Create 25 10 µL aliquots and store in -20 °C . These are your stock solutions which are stable for up to 3 months. You will dilute these aliquots for the experiment.
Create 25 10 µL aliquots and store in -20 °C . These are your stock solutions. You will dilute these aliquots for the experiment.
Reconstitute HCQ with 250 µL RNAse free H 2 O (filtered) to a final concentration of 46 millimolar (mM) .
Reconstitute MG-132 with 250 µL DMSO (filtered) to a final concentration of 42 millimolar (mM) .
Obtain 5 mg hydroxychloroquine sulfate (HCQ) from Sigma (#H0915-5MG)

For MG-132, create a 1:21 dilution of 42 mM stock solution in media to obtain a 2 millimolar (mM) dilution. You will add a volume of this to the cells at each time point. Perform dilutions needed for the experiment the day of , as dilutions of the stock solution should not be stored for more than 24 hours.

For HCQ, create a 1:21 dilution of 46 mM stock solution in H2O to obtain a 5 millimolar (mM) dilution. You will add a volume of this to the cells at each time point. Perform dilutions needed for the experiment the day of , as dilutions of the stock solution should not be stored for more than 24 hours.
creator: Christopher Bartley
